criteria.description: The DRG relation vocabulary includes a `specializes_from` relation for structural profile lineage, distinct from `delegates_to`.
criteria.notes: DRGRelation.SPECIALIZES_FROM added; value string verified; set-membership verified. Anti-finding confirmed: delegates_to not conflated.

criteria.description: Profile-lineage relation is distinct from runtime delegation; delegation consumers do not receive specializes_from edges.
criteria.notes: DRG traversal for delegation edges explicitly excludes SPECIALIZES_FROM; walk tests confirm isolation.

criteria.description: DRG validation accepts profile-to-profile lineage edges from shipped, organisation, and project graph fragments.
criteria.notes: All three layers accept specializes_from edges in their fragment; unknown-relation tests confirm rejection of unknown relations is separate.

criteria.description: DRG documentation and fixtures explain profile lineage semantics and its difference from delegation, enhancement, override, and replacement.
criteria.notes: Fixture pack explicitly demonstrates the lineage edge in merged DRG output; docs/explanation/doctrine-relationships.md added (confirmed by adversarial review I-8 mitigant).

criteria.description: Agent profile loading retains structured diagnostics for skipped profile files (source layer, path, profile ID if known, error summary).
criteria.notes: SkippedProfile dataclass carries layer, path, profile_id (Optional), error fields; frozen (immutable) per test_skipped_profile_is_frozen.

criteria.description: `AgentProfileRepository.list_all()` returns valid loaded profiles only; a dedicated diagnostic surface exposes skipped profiles without rescanning.
criteria.notes: list_all() excludes failed profiles; skipped_profiles property is separate; NFR-005 zero-diagnostics on shipped builtins confirmed.

criteria.description: `DoctrineService.agent_profiles` preserves profile load diagnostics for all configured layers after repository construction.
criteria.notes: Service-level preservation verified; diagnostics survive across multiple DoctrineService method calls.

criteria.description: `spec-kitty doctor doctrine` human output reports invalid or skipped agent profiles per source layer and pack.
criteria.notes: Human output includes an invalid-profile section for the degraded pack while still listing valid profiles.

criteria.description: `spec-kitty doctor doctrine --json` includes machine-readable invalid-profile diagnostics (layer, path, profile_id, error summary).
criteria.notes: Stable JSON fields confirmed via dict-emit test; false-healthy regression test passes.

criteria.description: A pack with invalid agent profiles is NOT presented as fully healthy when DRG node/edge counts are otherwise valid.
criteria.notes: PackHealth.healthy=False when invalid profiles present; inline-ref rejection also sets unhealthy and correct exit code.

criteria.description: `charter activate <kind> <id>` validates artifact ID existence for the requested kind before mutating activation state.
criteria.notes: Plan step validates ID; commit step never reached; error message names kind, missing ID, and recovery path.

criteria.description: Unknown activation IDs fail with actionable message (kind, missing ID, recovery path); no config write on failure.
criteria.notes: NFR-003 non-mutating invariant confirmed by CLI test checking config bytes unchanged. DIRECTIVE_013 tracker for doctor.py god-module split: see kitty-specs/org-doctrine-profile-integrity-closeout-01KT3G68/research/trackers-to-file.md#fr-012-doctor-py-split

criteria.description: Running `charter activate` without `--cascade` warns about referenced artifacts that were not cascaded.
criteria.notes: Skipped-reference warning surfaces referenced artifact kinds not cascaded; suggested recovery command included. DIRECTIVE_013 tracker for provenance typing deferral: see kitty-specs/org-doctrine-profile-integrity-closeout-01KT3G68/research/trackers-to-file.md#fr-013-provenance-typing

criteria.description: `charter activate <kind> <id> --cascade <scope>` preserves cascade scope and activates only selected referenced artifact kinds; `all` is the explicit all-kind shorthand.
criteria.notes: Cascade scope parsing and activation coverage confirmed across selected-kind and all-shorthand paths.

criteria.description: `charter deactivate <kind> <id> --cascade <scope>` deactivates only exclusively referenced artifacts and skips shared artifacts with a reason.
criteria.notes: Diamond shared-reference test confirms shared artifacts are skipped with reason; scope filter test confirms only selected kinds are deactivated.

criteria.description: Cascade activation and deactivation use the active DRG/catalog reference model rather than hardcoded per-kind special cases.
criteria.notes: Transitive cascade follows DRG requires/suggests edges; reference-relation discovery is graph-driven, not per-kind special-cased.

criteria.description: `build_operational_context()` returns populated OperationalContext at WP claim and runtime decision entry points when values are known.
criteria.notes: Production wiring confirmed at both implement-claim and next-decision entry points; no all-None context returned.

criteria.description: `require_active_profile()` and `require_active_role()` raise ContextPreconditionError with actionable messages when required fields are absent.
criteria.notes: Guard methods raise ContextPreconditionError (RuntimeError subclass) with actionable hint; precondition failure creates no worktree or status events (NFR-004).

criteria.description: OperationalContext symbols previously allowlisted as deferred work are removed from dead-symbol allowlists after production wiring.
criteria.notes: Two OperationalContext symbols (OperationalContext, build_operational_context) removed from allowlist after WP14 wired live callers; baseline updated from 6 to 4.

criteria.description: Known cleanup items from #1557 resolved: obsolete activation override code deleted, orphaned dead-symbol categories removed, stale `activate_cmd` export removed, FR-008 comment misattribution corrected.
criteria.notes: activate_cmd removed from __all__; sys.exit not present in activate module; activation routed through engine only.

criteria.description: Existing charter activation behavior from PR #1535 remains backward compatible for projects with no explicit activation restrictions.
criteria.notes: FR-021 explicitly labeled in test docstrings; no-restrictions path materializes default pack before appending, matching pre-#1535 behavior.

criteria.description: `charter context --include agent-profile:<id>` resolves and renders the named agent profile in human and `--json` output instead of failing with unsupported-kind error.
criteria.notes: Hyphenated kind name normalisation confirmed; profile rendered in both text and JSON output; unsupported-kind error no longer reached.

criteria.description: The `--include` selector parser normalizes hyphenated kind names to canonical doctrine kinds (e.g. `agent-profile` -> `agent_profile`).
criteria.notes: Both underscore and hyphenated forms resolve to same canonical kind; consistent with charter activate/deactivate.

criteria.description: The `charter context --include` help text advertises the agent-profile selector so supported kinds are discoverable.
criteria.notes: Help text confirmed to include agent-profile and template examples alongside existing directive/styleguide/section.

criteria.description: `charter list --all` lists every available artifact per kind across built-in, org-pack, and project layers, annotated by source layer.
criteria.notes: All three layers annotated; --all supersedes --show-available column header per test_all_supersedes_show_available_column_header.

criteria.description: `CharterPackManager.list_available()` includes org-pack and project artifacts in addition to built-ins, removing the built-in-only scan.
criteria.notes: Multi-layer listing confirmed; each artifact carries its source layer annotation; built-in-only scan removed.

criteria.description: Canonical kind vocabulary used by `charter context --include`, `activate`, `deactivate`, and `list` resolves through a single mapping rather than per-command re-declaration.
criteria.notes: Single canonical kind vocabulary module; all command surfaces route through it; facade re-export verified by identity.

criteria.description: Augmentation/lineage relationships (`overrides`/`enhances`, `specializes_from`) expressible for Directive, Toolguide, mission step contract, and mission type kinds.
criteria.notes: All newly-covered kinds confirmed eligible; field projection to DRG edge confirmed for directives and profile lineage.

criteria.description: Field-merge semantics for topology-bearing kinds under `enhances` are defined in ADR `2026-05-16-1`: field-merge vs replace specified, action-sequence ordering and step I/O preserved; `overrides` is full replacement.
criteria.notes: ADR 2026-05-16-1 at architecture/3.x/adr/2026-05-16-1-doctrine-layer-merge-semantics.md governs semantics; topology-integrity assertions confirmed by test_enhances_rejects_silent_step_drop.

criteria.description: DRG auto-emit table and pack-validator augmentation set cover all augmentation-eligible kinds and derive from a single shared source.
criteria.notes: Single shared _AUGMENTATION_PLURAL_TO_KIND source confirmed; eligible-set formula verified (all ArtifactKinds minus template, plus mission_type).

criteria.description: Pack validator applies intent-aware collision behavior uniformly to newly-covered kinds at parity with original five.
criteria.notes: All three intent behaviors confirmed for newly-covered kinds; lineage relation excluded from intent-advisory per test_fragment_intent_ignores_lineage_relation.

criteria.description: Mission-type augmentation delivered by expanding `_ORG_DRG_CANONICAL_KINDS` to include mission types; `_ORG_DRG_KIND_ALIASES` and contract-test sweep updated in lockstep.
criteria.notes: Canonical 9-kind universe (8 ArtifactKinds + mission_type) confirmed; lockstep drift guard passes; mission-type fragment augmentation fixture validates.

criteria.description: Doctrine templates are discoverable: a listing surface enumerates available templates across mission/tier sources, annotated by source tier.
criteria.notes: Discovery surface enumerates templates with tier annotation; deterministic output; same-name disambiguation across missions confirmed.

criteria.description: `template` kind addressable through canonical vocabulary; `charter list --all` includes templates; `charter context --include template:<id>` resolves named template.
criteria.notes: Template kind row appears in charter list --all; template context inclusion resolves by mission-qualified ID; template absent without --all confirmed.

criteria.description: Charter activation and context entry points catch typed `CharterPackConfigError` and present code + remediation as fail-closed error; no activation state mutated; symbol no longer dead.
criteria.evidence: tests/charter/test_pack_context.py (CharterPackConfigError raises on malformed config) + tests/architectural/test_no_dead_symbols.py (CharterPackConfigError removed from allowlist after wiring)
criteria.notes: CharterPackConfigError has live external callers (activation/context CLI entry points); removed from category_b allowlist baseline (275->271 in _baselines.yaml).

criteria.description: In-scope inherited dead-symbol debt cleared: 7 stale `_SYMBOL_ALLOWLIST` entries removed; `CharterPackConfigError` wired; charter sub-app exports cleaned.
criteria.notes: 5 entries removed from category_b (CharterPackConfigError + 2 lifecycle_events + 2 events.py re-exports); upstream status_service 5-symbol debt tracked via DIRECTIVE_013 tracker (see kitty-specs/org-doctrine-profile-integrity-closeout-01KT3G68/research/trackers-to-file.md#upstream-status-service-debt). Two git/lanes out-of-scope offenders tracked by #1588.

negative_invariants.description: delegates_to edges must not appear as profile lineage edges
charter activate must not mutate config on unknown-ID failure
cascade deactivation must not remove shared artifacts
